The caliber of the people in the bubble home park ain't exactly the same as the caliber of people who camp out on boats for three months. And so we'll go back to having a boat. And so they got another boat. Now what I have just described is has unimaginable to the majority of people outside this room that it has, is unimaginable, has anything you could think of that would be unimaginable. It is inconceivable that you would have a boat, not have a boat, get a motor home, decide you don't like the motor home, get rid of the motor home, and go get another boat. You've just described something you might as well have described them, sprout wings, fly into Mars, bringing back blue rocks in their mouths. I mean, it's just inconceivable to them choosing the place you live. Now not so much as it used to be, true, but still. Over 50% of the people die where they're born. Some come back. So I did. Some were like elephants. You know, when they know it's getting towards the end, they come back, right? But it's over 30% never leave. Tell it. They stay where they were planted, which pretty much was random in the first place. And the idea of just pack it up and going, no matter how miserable and broke they are where they are, the idea of packing up and just going somewhere is inconceivable to them. So I've had the conversation many times, because when I left here and moved to Phoenix, it was a five. Well, if you count the three days I was trapped in my apartment. It's an eight day decision. And we're gone. And I'd never been there. The decision was, how far away from snow can you get and not be in California? That was the decision. I'm serious. That was the decision. So look at a map. How far away from winter can I get? But I know I don't want to be in California, Arizona. That was it. I just told you the whole thinking process, right? And arguably, by the way, if I'd been a little bit more deliberative about it, I would have gone to Nevada. That has a state income tax. And I wouldn't be here. Because I would have hit my enough is enough number about 9, 10, 11 years ago. And somebody else would be here talking to you. But anyway, so I've had this conversation many times, because I picked up and moved fairly quickly. I sold a business in a week. I mean, I was gone. After three days trapped in my apartment with my wife, unable to get out, this cannot happen again. Or murder will ensue. And I wasn't confident that I was going to emerge as the murderer. So I would call that a toss-up. So this could not be permitted to happen even one more time. When you cannot see the roof of your car for the snow, you got to go. So when I had a conversation with people, it's just unimaginable to them. The speed of the decision, the not having been there, this is beyond their possible comprehension. So all of it that we sort of now take for granted, that you take for granted, that is part of your belief system now, that so where's the British couple that lost all the weight? Oh, there you are. So how much did you lose? OK, so how much in sizes? Four sizes. In how many months? I said, good for you. And how many dress sizes, sir, did you lose? I don't know. The measurement, 30 hits and 34. OK, good, very good. So they were at a seminar in the UK. And I guess I was the final nudge, because I made probably an impolite remark about weight. Yeah, the combined diet and exercise program in one, which is you place the palm against the table and you push your fat ass back before you eat a second healthy. It's actually the only one that works. And when you're saying it in the presence of overweight people, it's rude. And I imagine for the British, it's probably even more rude, because they tend to be a polite people unlike us. And I've said many times, by the way, because you don't know, the British audiences are so much better than the American audiences, so much better. If the damn place wasn't so far away and so small, I would infinitely rather work. So anyway, so they were ready, I guess, and got a nudge. And they've now dropped all this weight. And they look fabulous, right? But, and so, I don't know, did you do it on your own? Did you join some program? Did you did it on your own? All on your own. Just did it. You understand how unimaginable that is to most people, right? They cannot, you understand, they cannot conceive of it. So then they would immediately start going back into all the stuff we talked about this morning, while they're genetic freaks, there's some explanation for how they are able to do this. And I can't do it. It is unimaginable. So this whole issue of possibility, the acceptance of possibility cannot be assumed by you in your advertising, marketing, and selling. It can't be assumed. So like Susan, if you look at your stuff about someone becoming a voiceover talent, there's an assumption on your part that they believe that's possible. I haven't even got the personal possibility, just possible. And for many people, the assumption's erroneous. So what do they really believe? They believe there's a part of that story that's not being told, that actually is the explanation.
